publications.title: Molecular determinants of cross-strain influenza A virus recognition by αβ T cell receptors.
publications.authors: Quiñones-Parra SM, Gras S, Nguyen THO, Farenc C, Szeto C, Rowntree LC, Chaurasia P, Sant S, Boon ACM, Jayasinghe D, Rimmelzwaan GF, Petersen J, Doherty PC, Uldrich AP, Littler DR, Rossjohn J, Kedzierska K.
publications.executive_summary: Cross-reactive αβ T cell receptors that recognize multiple variants of the influenza A virus nucleoprotein epitope NP418-426 presented by HLA-B*35:01 were identified through screening of 12 naturally occurring peptide variants that have circulated since 1918, including contemporary A/H5N1 strains. Six crystal structures of TCR–HLA-B*35:01/NP418 complexes revealed that broadly cross-reactive TCRs achieve epitope variant recognition by engaging invariant and conserved peptide–MHC features while positioning variable interactions away from hypervariable peptide positions, thereby maintaining functional recognition despite antigen sequence drift. This cross-reactivity comes at the cost of reduced antigen sensitivity and low avidity, a trade-off validated in primary human CD8+ T cell lines where CD8 coreceptor binding is required to stabilize weak interactions. The findings illuminate the molecular basis for broad TCR recognition of a rapidly evolving viral target and highlight how low-avidity cross-reactive responses contribute to sustained protective immunity against influenza strains with sequence-divergent epitope variants.
